observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor mandate the Municipal
Accountant to review previous procurements on PPE to obtain the necessary data
about the LGU properties, furnish the same information to the Inventory Committee
and prepare and maintain PPE Ledger Cards.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the designated
Property Officer to prepare and maintain Property Cards for every property
procured by the LGU and reconcile his records with Accounting Records monthly.

Lastly, we recommended that the Inventory Committee include all vital data
about the properties covered in the physical count and include the same in the
RPCPPE to facilitate confirmation with accounting records.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. Identify and trace the causes of said unreconciled balances of the intra-
agency accounts and effect its reconciliation.
b. Maintain and update regularly the subsidiary ledgers of the said accounts
to facilitate early detection of errors, and determine if the total of the
individual accounts tally with the general ledger.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare a certified list of
taxpayers based on the Real Property Tax Assessment Roll from the Municipal
Assessor and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant. Subsequently, require
the Municipal Accountant to record the amount of RPT collectibles at the start of
every year to show the correct balances of RPT and SET Receivables in the financial
statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor institute appropriate legal means
to recover from Ms. Julia C. Gaza the missing funds of the LGU including the
settlement of her unliquidated cash advances. Henceforth, require the incumbent
Municipal Treasurer to submit a Cash Position Report to monitor the cash in her
custody and to require the Municipal Accountant to make a report in case the
Municipal Treasurer fails to submit daily her Report of Collections and Deposits.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instigate actions to mandate Ms.
Corazon C. Gervacio and Ms. Morita Sevillano to restitute immediately their
remaining cash shortages in the amount of P712,882.85 and P280,118.34,
respectively, and deposit the entire amount to the LGU’s bank account.

We also recommended that the new OIC Municipal Treasurer require all
Collectors to remit to her their collections intact and daily. Further, verify the
accuracy of each Report of Collections and Deposits of each Collector through
comparison of the accountable forms in their custody. Constant monitoring and
control in the issuances of the unused accountable forms is also recommended.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer (1) mandatorily require all
Revenue Collectors to prepare Monthly RAAF for consolidation and submission to
the offices concerned within the prescribed period; (2) inspect accountable forms in
the custody of the collectors upon remittance, and; (3) store all unused accountable
forms in a safe place.

We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan enact a Resolution for the
reactivation of the current depository account on Performance Challenge for proper
disposition of its remaining funds such as the immediate transfer of the funds to the
general fund. We also recommended the Municipal Mayor decide whether to use or
close the current depository account on Kalahi CIDDS to avoid further bank charges.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require each concerned
Department Head to fast-track the implementation of programs and projects funded
by the National Government Agencies in accordance with the timeline provided for
in the MOA. For subsequent fund transfer, require the concerned Department Head
to obtain copies of duly approved notarized MOAs from Source Agencies upon
receipt of the fund. Also, require them to submit the Liquidation Reports and other
documents to the Source Agency immediately upon completion of the projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Council
(MPDC), through the Municipal Mayor as Chairman, include in the Annual
Investment Plan only, the projects which can readily be implemented by the LGU
during the year taking into consideration the timetable on its implementation and
requiring complete documentation prior to inclusion in the AIP.

Also, we recommended that the Municipal Engineer implement fully the
projects contained in the AIP, through proper scheduling and prioritization of the
projects to be implemented, to ensure optimal utilization of the 20% Development
Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Development Council through the
Mayor as Chairman and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer, include
only in the Annual Investment Plan of the Municipality projects with social, economic
and environmental development outcomes which are investment or capital in nature
so as to maximize the use of the 20% Development Fund and provide the citizens of
the municipality with the benefits that the projects can provide.

We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal
Accountant to utilize the 20% Development Fund for purposes that are outlined in
DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-01 dated February 22, 2017 and
those identified in the AIP.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unexpended prior years
LDRRMF amounting to P1,023,947.71 to Special Trust Fund Account as required
by regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the GAD Focal
Person to ensure the full implementation of the PPAs listed in the GAD Plan and that
sufficient budget for such activities are allocated. Also, secure from the other offices
the report on implementation of the mainstreamed GAD related activities and include
the same in the GAD Accomplishment Report.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the SWM Board to
strictly enforce the provisions of R.A. No. 9003, specifically the complete
implementation of the planned programs, projects and activities stated in the SWM
Plan including the proper methods of solid waste segregation, collection, and disposal,
to ensure safe and sanitary management of solid waste in the municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to require the Local Council for
the Protection of Children (LCPC) in the municipality to prepare the prescribed
Annual Work and Financial Plan on prioritized programs and activities based on the
needs of the children in the community.

We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer ensure that the total
funds appropriated for programs, projects and activities to be undertaken by the
different departments of the LGU which will be attributed for LCPC should be
equivalent to one percent (1%) of the IRA of the current year.

We recommended that the Local School Board incorporate in its budget in the
Special Education Fund programs and activities intended for Early Childhood Care
Development.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant submit the missing 193 DVs and its supporting documents and that the municipal officials and employees concerned submit monthly financial reports and other documents within the due date prescribed by law.
observations.managementAction: Only 15 DVs were submitted. The Municipal Treasurer and Accountant submit the required reports and documents as these become available.
observations.reason: Still retrieving the remaining DVs to avoid the issuance of suspensions; submission of some documents delayed and incomplete.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer with supporting documents; conduct physical inventory of the local road network and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network (RPCLRN); and the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant maintain the required Local Road Network Property and Ledger Cards, respectively.
observations.managementAction: The Mayor already directed the concerned Department Head to implement the projects and provide required documents; Inventory committee to include the conduct of the physical inventory of PPEs and local road network.
observations.reason: Some infrastructure projects were implemented while other projects are still on-going and documentation in support of balances are being prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer ensure that all collections are recorded in the appropriate fund and deposited to the correct account and that the Municipal Accountant be informed of any adjustments made in the cashbooks and transfers made from one fund to another. Also, review thoroughly each Report of Collections and Deposits before submitting the same to the Accounting Office.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant should review in detail the Reports of Collections and Deposits submitted by the Municipal Treasurer and inform the latter of any noted discrepancies. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer will investigate causes of negative balances and prepare necessary adjustments and conduct monthly reconciliation of cashbook and general ledger.
observations.reason: Investigations and reconciliations are ongoing; negative balances under review and adjustments to be made where necessary.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned Department Heads to accelerate implementation of the projects, submit liquidation reports and copies of duly signed and notarized MOAs to the source agencies and to COA.
observations.managementAction: The concerned Department Heads were directed to implement the projects and submit liquidation reports. Copies of the duly signed MOAs should be obtained and made available.
observations.reason: Some projects were implemented and liquidation reports were submitted for those projects; other projects remained unimplemented and documentation not yet submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Development Council include in the AIP only capital development projects and exclude administrative expenses; and the Municipal Engineer should implement the projects per AIP.
observations.managementAction: Municipal management to monitor project implementation and re-align funds where necessary; projects prioritized for implementation.
observations.reason: Funds were sometimes accumulated to finance bigger infrastructure projects; some administrative expenses were charged to 20% DF and some projects remain to be implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly utilize the MDRRMF for disaster preparedness activities, reconcile their records to arrive at correct balances of prior years' MDRRMF and implement mitigation measures.
observations.managementAction: They have started to enforce measures and utilize available funds for disaster mitigation and preparedness. The Budget Officer and Accountant are to reconcile records.
observations.reason: Some mitigation projects implemented; scarcity of funds hinders full implementation and some balances from prior years were accumulated for major projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommend strict implementation and prioritize improvement of school facilities and maximize the use of SEF for its intended purposes and stop payments of expenditures not authorized to be paid from SEF.
observations.managementAction: As of CY 2017 the Mayor as Chairman of the School Board include in the SEF budget projects and programs for improvement of school facilities; stop payment of non-authorized expenditures from SEF.
observations.reason: Some SEF funds were still used for sports and similar activities; management will maximize use of SEF for school improvement.

observations.recommendation: We recommend acquisition of land for a dumpsite and development of sanitary landfill and accelerate procurement and construction of MSWM facilities.
observations.managementAction: Municipality identified a lot as top priority for sanitary landfill acquisition; negotiations with land owner are ongoing; LGU continued rental of dumpsite lot while acquisition is in process.
observations.reason: Procurement/acquisition of lot still in negotiation; construction of facilities pending; rental of dumpsite continued; implementation delayed.

observations.recommendation: We recommend strict compliance with procurement law and documentation, obtain reasonable price quotes and supporting documents to avoid suspensions and disallowances.
observations.managementAction: Management to adhere to procurement processes and secure justifying documents and alternative modes of procurement where applicable. Also, BAC Secretariat to submit required supporting documents to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the timely submission to COA of Purchase Orders, contracts, inspection reports and other supporting documents within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: Purchasing officers complied and submitted documents; copies of contracts, purchase orders and inspection reports were being compiled for submission.
observations.reason: Some POs and supporting documents were submitted; voluminous transactions and distance caused delay in submitting all documents within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that purchase orders indicate delivery date and terms and that suppliers be held to the delivery dates with penalties for late delivery where applicable.
observations.managementAction: Management instructed procurement personnel to always include delivery date and other required information in POs to ensure enforceability.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that procurements for common-use supplies be coursed through PS and if unavailable, secure Certificate of Non-Availability and use alternative modes per IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: LGU now resorts to PS procurement where possible and obtains Certificate of Non-Availability when necessary and resorts to alternative modes per IRR of RA 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to stop the practice of reimbursement for procurement and adhere strictly to procurement law to ensure reasonableness and existence of goods and services procured.
observations.managementAction: Management to promote transparency and require procurements to follow required procurement procedure; reimbursements limited to small value items with proper justification.
observations.reason: Some transactions were prior year transactions and retrieval of supporting documents is difficult; LGU enjoined to promote transparency and follow procurement procedures.

observations.recommendation: We recommend compliance with the deadlines set under Section 347 of RA 7160, Section 100 of PD 1445 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations in the submission of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The agency rendered the required reports and the accounts mentioned earlier were submitted; management to ensure timely submission in future.

observations.recommendation: We recommend strict adherence to remittance and deposit rules; Municipal Treasurer to require collectors to remit their collections daily or next working day and deposit to authorized depository bank to prevent misuse or loss; follow Section 32, Chapter II of the Revised Cash Examination Manual.
observations.managementAction: They were required to comply; demand letters issued; some recoveries and deposits were made; new procedures enforced by acting municipal treasurer.
observations.reason: Some collections were loaned out or mishandled by employees and some accounts involved employees transferred or separated making full settlement difficult; follow-up ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommend compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 in timely submission of required reports.
observations.managementAction: Municipal LDRRMO and Accountant have started to render the LDRRMF reports and comply with the related provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.reason: Some reports were submitted; continuing compliance required.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the agency to regularly furnish/submit copies of all the above-mentioned documents/reports to the Audit Team for prompt audit of its transactions.
observations.managementAction: They have furnished several required documents and are in the process of submitting additional required documents to COA.
observations.reason: Some documents submitted; others pending compilation and submission.

observations.recommendation: We recommend establishment of an Inventory Committee, conduct physical inventory of all properties at least once a year, establish property identification and coding, maintain Acknowledgment Receipts of Equipment (ARE), prepare inventory reports, reconcile inventory with financial statements, identify accountable persons for losses and maintain/update property cards and PPE ledger cards.
observations.managementAction: The Mayor, municipal treasurer and municipal accountant to constitute inventory committee and implement the recommended measures; some inventory activities initiated.
observations.reason: Some measures implemented such as creation of committee and initiation of inventories; full reconciliation and documentation pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly adhere with the required payment of checks directly to suppliers except for small value items that could be paid out of petty cash fund. Stop the practice of reimbursement and strict adherence to the procurement law is hereby enjoined to promote transparency in all LGUs transactions and to afford assurance on the reasonableness of the prices being paid for and existence of the procured goods and services.
observations.reason: Due to urgent needs of supplies by some offices that tend them to purchase directly to payees from nearby stores.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and other persons concerned to submit immediately the agency’s accounts and all financial records mentioned earlier. Henceforth, the LGU is required to comply within the deadline set under Section 347 of RA 7160, Section 100 of PD 1445 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations in the submission of accounts.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant explained that delay submission of accounts mainly caused by the long unclaimed payments/payrolls and other financial records mentioned earlier.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require all the accountable officers to strictly comply with the rules and regulations on the remittance and deposit of collections. The Municipal Treasurer shall strictly supervise and employ system of control over her collectors and to require them to remit their collections daily or next working day and that the latter shall deposit the same regularly to their authorized depository bank to prevent the possible misuse or loss of funds.
observations.managementAction: The collectors remained lax on the regular remittance of collections, of which the Municipal Treasurer could neither stop the remittance of collections nor issue accountable forms to them because they failed to remit their collections. Henceforth no additional accountable forms shall be issued to those Collectors who failed to remit their collections.
observations.reason: Collectors' laxity on the regular remittance of collections, of which the Municipal Treasurer could neither stop the remittance of collections nor issue accountable forms to them in the municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that only necessary travels be allowed; if possible, travel of this kind shall be authorized only if extremely and urgently necessary, beneficial to the municipality and will require minimum cost.
observations.managementAction: Had conducted their year-end activity in Tacloban City.

observations.recommendation: We request the Local Chief executive to require the accountable officers including the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with rules and regulations on the remittance and deposit of collections on time. This must be carried out strictly to ensure that none of the funds of government is lost or misused.
observations.managementAction: Accountable Officers remained non-compliant. Two collectors were relieved from duties.
observations.reason: Collectors remained non-compliant; some collectors relieved from duties.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to: a) submit to the Auditor’s Office the contracts, POs and Acceptance Inspection Reports (AIRs) within five (5) days after perfection to facilitate timely auditorial review; b) ensure that the terms, conditions and period of delivery of the contracts, POs and AIR are clearly indicated to facilitate proper assessment of the performance by suppliers and contractors of their obligations.
observations.managementAction: Had conducted regular submission; however, some documents were not submitted to the Audit team timely.
observations.reason: Due to laxity of concerned officials/employees; hence, reiterated this finding in this year’s audit report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to submit the Annual Procurement Plan to COA on the prescribed time for assessment and evaluation purposes.
observations.reason: Due to laxity of concerned official/employee; hence, reiterated this finding in this year’s audit report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant submit the
missing 193 DVs and its supporting documents to avoid the issuance of audit
suspensions. Also, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor require all officials and
employees who have a direct hand in the processing, approval and payment of the
transactions to submit paid disbursement vouchers and other financial reports within
the due date prescribed by law.

We recommend the following courses of actions:
(a) The Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer with the complete description and cost segregation of road
components for road projects.
(b) The Inventory committee include in the conduct of the physical inventory of
PPEs the local road networks and the preparation of Report on the Physical
Count of the Road Network (RPCLRN);
(c) The Municipal Treasurer and Accountant maintain the required Local Road
Network Property and Ledger Cards, respectively.

We recommend the following courses of actions:
1. The Municipal Treasurer ensure that all collections are recorded in the
appropriate fund and deposited to the correct depository account to avoid
interchange in recording of transactions. Inform the Municipal Accountant
of any adjustments made in the cashbooks and transfers made from one fund
to another. Also, review thoroughly each Report of Collections and Deposits
before submitting the same to the Accounting Office.
2. The Municipal Accountant review in detail the Reports of Collections and
Deposits submitted by the Municipal Treasurer and inform the latter of any
noted discrepancies.
3. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer investigate the
causes of the negative balances and prepare the necessary adjustments where
necessary. Also, conduct a monthly reconciliation of the entries in the
cashbook and general ledger and prepare the necessary adjustments on any
unreconciled items immediately.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned Department
Heads to accelerate the implementation of programs and projects funded by the
National Government in accordance with the timeline provided in the MOA. Copies
of all duly signed MOA should be obtained from the source agencies and be made
available to concerned offices including COA.
Also, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant submit the Liquidation
Reports to the Source Agencies on implemented programs/projects and return any
unutilized fund immediately to avoid misuse of the same.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor monitor strictly the
implementation of projects that are included in the AIP and utilize the 20%
Development Fund only for socio-economic, environmental development projects.
Also, we recommend that the Municipal Development Council include in the AIP only
socio-economic and development projects and exclude administrative expenses.

We recommend that the MDRRM Council utilize fully its Mitigation Fund and
the accumulated balances from prior years for disaster mitigation and preparedness
activities in order to strengthen the capability of the Municipality to cope with adverse
effects of disasters and improve the safety and well-being of the residents in times of
calamity. We also recommend that the Budget Officer and Accountant reconcile
their respective records in order to arrive at the correct balances of prior years’
MDRRMF.

We recommend that the Municipal School Board, through the Mayor as
Chairman, include in the SEF budget projects and activities that are considered
priorities in the use of SEF specifically on improvement of school facilities. Also,
maximize the use of the SEF in accordance with the purpose for which it was created
and stop payments of expenditures which are not authorized to be paid from SEF.

We recommend that the Solid Waste Management Board consider as top
priority the acquisition of lot to be used for their dumpsite in order to develop a
sanitary landfill site and construct solid waste management facilities. Also,
implement the MSWM Plan immediately and completely.

We recommend that the Municipal mayor approve procurement through “shopping”
only if the vouchers are supported with duly approved BAC resolution justifying and
recommending the alternative mode of procurement. Also, the disbursements should
be supported with all the required documents to avoid the suspension and/or
disallowance of the transactions in audit.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor mandate the Municipal Treasurer and
Accountant to submit the Disbursement Vouchers and the supporting documents
pertaining to the procurement of vehicle and motorcycles in the total amount of
P978,559.82.
We further recommend that the BAC Chairman, through the BAC Secretariat, submit
copies of approved contracts, purchase order and its supporting documents to COA
within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: In order to assure the reliability of the PPE balances, the following measures are recommended: 1. Create an Inventory Committee representative of the local chief executive as chairman and the general services officer or municipal treasurer and supply officer of the department or office concerned, as members; 2. Conduct physical inventory of all properties at least once a year; 3. Establish property identification and coding; 4. Maintain Acknowledgment Receipt of Equipment (ARE) to establish the official accountable for the property; 5. Prepare inventory reports; 6. Reconcile inventory report with balances reflected in the financial statements; 7. Identify persons accountable for the loss of property and effect adjustments if necessary; and 8. Maintain and update property cards and PPE ledger cards.
